name: Moncayo from Cueva de Ágreda Trail
name_original: Pico Moncayo desde Cuevas de Ágreda
description: Hike the challenging 7.4-mile Pico Moncayo desde Cuevas de Ágreda route to the highest peak of the Iberian System with stunning views.
region_intro: Hiking around Cueva De Ágreda offers access to the mountainous landscapes of the Moncayo Natural Park. The region is characterized by its varied terrain, including forested slopes, high-altitude trails, and open valleys. Hikers can expect routes that traverse different elevations, providing diverse perspectives of the natural environment. Best hiking trails around Cueva De Ágreda The most popular hiking route is Moncayo Circular Trail, a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 50 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers varied terrain and panoramic views of the Moncayo massif. Another top favourite among local hikers is Moncayo from Cueva de Ágreda Trail, a difficult 7.4 miles (11.9 km) path. This trail features significant elevation gain as it ascends towards the Moncayo peak. Local hikers also love the Cabezo de la Mata from Fuente de los Frailes — circular through the Moncayo Natural Park, a 4.4 miles (7.1 km) trail leading through the Moncayo Natural Park, often completed in about 2 hours 27 minutes. Hiking around Cueva De Ágreda is defined by mountainous terrain, forested paths, and high-altitude views. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Cueva De Ágreda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 1500 hikers have used komoot to explore Cueva De Ágreda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Cueva De Ágreda?
A: There are over 50 hiking trails around Cueva De Ágreda, offering a wide range of options within the Moncayo Natural Park. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate walks to more challenging ascents.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Cueva De Ágreda?
A: Yes, Cueva De Ágreda offers several easy hiking trails. While the region is known for its mountainous terrain, there are 8 routes classified as easy, providing accessible options for those new to hiking or looking for a more relaxed experience.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Cueva De Ágreda area?
A: Yes, the region features several circular routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971939287/moncayo-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.7 miles (10.8 km) long and offers varied terrain with panoramic views of the Moncayo massif. Another popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981868281/cabezo-de-la-mata-from-fuente-de-los-frailes-circular-through-the-moncayo-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de la Mata from Fuente de los Frailes — circular through the Moncayo Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I expect to see along the trails?
A: The trails around Cueva De Ágreda traverse the Moncayo Natural Park, offering diverse natural beauty. You can explore forested slopes and high-altitude paths. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5943898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soriano Natural Water Trail&lt;/a&gt; and various viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5573920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Tío Fresquito&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5304172" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Moncayo&lt;/a&gt;, providing stunning vistas of the massif.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Absolutely. For experienced trekkers, the region offers challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057568211/moncayo-from-cueva-de-agreda-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo from Cueva de Ágreda Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 7.4 miles (11.9 km) path ascending towards the Moncayo peak. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40309588" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de los Frailes – Moncayo Summit loop from Fuente de los Frailes&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 1000 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Cueva De Ágreda?
A: The Moncayo Natural Park, where Cueva De Ágreda is located, is generally best for hiking from late spring through early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is typically more stable, and trails are clear of snow, especially at higher elevations. Winter hiking is possible but requires appropriate gear and experience due to snow and colder conditions.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, there are options for refreshments and accommodation. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario del Moncayo Restaurant and Hostel&lt;/a&gt; is a notable spot. Additionally, you can find refuges and shelters like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6255491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Majada Baja Refuge&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288064" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdemanzano Nature Center and Shelter&lt;/a&gt;, which can be useful for longer treks.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Cueva De Ágreda?
A: The hiking routes in Cueva De Ágreda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 150 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the stunning panoramic views of the Moncayo massif, and the sense of immersion in nature within the natural park.

Q: Is it possible to reach the Moncayo Summit from Cueva De Ágreda?
A: Yes, reaching the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo Summit&lt;/a&gt; is a popular goal for hikers in the area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057568211/moncayo-from-cueva-de-agreda-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo from Cueva de Ágreda Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route specifically designed for this ascent, offering significant elevation gain as it leads to the peak.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes in this region?
A: Hiking durations in Cueva De Ágreda vary significantly depending on the trail's difficulty and length. For example, a moderate route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971939287/moncayo-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 4 hours 50 minutes, while shorter, easier paths can be completed in 2-3 hours. More difficult ascents to the Moncayo peak can take 5 hours or more.

Q: Are there any specific peaks or viewpoints worth visiting?
A: Beyond the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo Summit&lt;/a&gt;, several other peaks and viewpoints offer spectacular vistas. These include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5573920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Tío Fresquito&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de la Mata&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4708682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Negrilla Summit&lt;/a&gt;. Each provides a unique perspective of the Moncayo Natural Park's rugged beauty.
tips: At this point the two paths by which you can climb Moncayo from Cuevas de Ágreda come together. Normally, you go up the one on the right, through the Barranco del Colladico.

Located at an altitude of 2,314.3 meters, we arrive at Pico Moncayo, within the Natural Park of the same name. The panoramic views are splendid, but the route to the peak is also splendid, passing through the beautiful oak forest. Do not forget your coat, in any season the wind blows in Moncayo!

faqs.question: How challenging is the Pico Moncayo desde Cuevas de Ágreda trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is considered difficult and intense. It involves a significant and constant ascent with over 970 meters of positive elevation gain over approximately 11.87 kilometers. While not technically difficult in terms of requiring specialized climbing, it demands good physical condition due to the sustained uphill effort. Higher elevations can have loose terrain or snow, and the summit is often exposed to strong winds.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: Based on Komoot's data, the estimated duration for this hike is around 4 hours and 57 minutes (17,859 seconds). However, this can vary depending on your fitness level, weather conditions, and time spent enjoying the views at the summit.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the ascent to Pico Moncayo from Cuevas de Ágreda?
faqs.answer: The initial section follows an evident track through an oak grove (Robledal) alongside the Trasmoncayo river. As you gain altitude, the path narrows, becomes steeper, and the forest gives way to open slopes. Higher up, especially past the Trasmoncayo river source, the terrain can be looser and more complex, potentially with snow. The descent, particularly around Collado de Pasalobos, features 'very broken' sections with loose and steep rocks, requiring caution.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or views along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse scenery, starting with pleasant views of the oak forest and the sound of the Trasmoncayo river. As you ascend, broad vistas of the mountainside unfold. You'll traverse the Barranco del Colladico. From the Moncayo Summit (Pico San Miguel), you'll experience exceptional panoramic views, including the Soria plateau and distant Picos de Urbión and Sierra Cebollera.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike Pico Moncayo from Cuevas de Ágreda?
faqs.answer: While beautiful year-round, the peak is frequently snow-capped in winter, offering a stunning spectacle but increasing difficulty. For less challenging conditions, late spring to early autumn is generally recommended. Be prepared for strong winds and variable weather at higher elevations regardless of the season.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the hike from Cuevas de Ágreda?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from Cuevas de Ágreda. Specific parking information isn't detailed, but generally, you would look for designated parking areas within or near the village that provide access to the trailhead. As this route is less crowded than others, parking is usually more accessible.

faqs.question: Is the Pico Moncayo desde Cuevas de Ágreda trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails within the Parque Natural del Moncayo, but they should always be kept on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Be mindful of the challenging terrain and potential for loose rocks, which might be difficult for some dogs.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the Parque Natural del Moncayo?
faqs.answer: As of now, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail or access the Parque Natural del Moncayo. However, it's always advisable to check the official website of the park or local tourism office for any updated regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route follows sections of the GR 86 (Sendero Ibérico Soriano), a recognized long-distance walking route. It also intersects with other local paths such as the Circular del Moncayo, Subida invernal, Senda a la Peña San Miguel, and Camino de Los Esquiñones.

faqs.question: Are there any unique features or historical aspects to look out for on the trail?
faqs.answer: Moncayo holds a mythical and legendary status, having inspired authors like Gustavo Adolfo Bécquer. You might also encounter remains of aircraft that crashed on the mountain years ago, adding a poignant historical layer to the landscape. This ascent route from the Soria side (Cuevas de Ágreda) is generally less crowded, offering a more tranquil hiking experience compared to the Aragon side.

faqs.question: What kind of weather conditions should I expect on Moncayo, and what should I wear?
faqs.answer: The higher elevations of Moncayo are often exposed to strong winds and can experience inclement weather, even outside of winter. Be prepared for rapid changes in conditions. It's recommended to wear layers, including windproof and waterproof outer shells, sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support, and bring essentials like a hat, gloves, and sun protection.
